name: browse
tagline: Persistent headless-Chromium CLI for QA testing, screenshots, form/dialog automation, and before/after page diffing.
verdict: Exceptionally detailed and actionable, with genuinely good security design: an explicit untrusted-content firewall for scraped page text, a deny-default CDP allowlist, and safe proxy-credential handling. Weak points: no stated scope exclusions, the same undisclosed shared side effects (CLAUDE.md edits/commits, telemetry, migration) as its sibling gstack.

highlights.summary: Opt-in telemetry (duration/outcome/repo name) posted to a gstack endpoint via the shared preamble; off by default but a baked-in destination.
description: 'Fast headless browser for QA testing and site dogfooding.' doesn't disclose the preamble's CLAUDE.md edits/commits, migration, or telemetry.
No 'NOT for X' statement anywhere despite very broad browser-automation scope.
